method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/dhs (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace, livesite-version: 22) and confirmed DHS has NO self-service portal — its transactions route through NYC311 ('Homeless Person Assistance') and in-person intake centers. Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Homeless Services (DHS)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 23 assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' shared chassis (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace); no service portal — transactions route through NYC311
meta.netnew: OutreachRequest — request street outreach for a person experiencing homelessness (today only via NYC311)
meta.tagline: DHS publishes a rich, open picture of homelessness but gives New Yorkers no API to act on it.
meta.headline: The data is wide open and there is no service layer at all — the human-services action routes through a NYC311 phone call.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' CMS (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace); no DHS-specific or modern platform
scorecard_notes.siteApi: Informational site only; no content API
scorecard_notes.openData: 23 datasets led by the flagship DHS Daily Report (87k views); strong census/directory/facility coverage
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core entities well modeled but aggregate-only snapshots; no live per-shelter roster or individual records
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write API anywhere; every action is a 311 call, 911, or in-person intake
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface of any kind
scorecard_notes.ownership: Reference data lives on NYC's Socrata tenant; the stand-in service channel (NYC311) belongs to another agency, not DHS

headline_findings: DHS is an observational-data agency: its informational site is the shared NYC.gov 'Livesite' chassis (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace) and it ships no application of its own — every transaction happens on NYC311, 911, or at an in-person intake center.

The reference data is unusually OPEN: 23 NYC Open Data datasets led by the flagship DHS Daily Report (the daily shelter census, DHS's single most-viewed asset at 87k views), plus drop-in centers, DHS contacts/intake centers, buildings and individual census, the Shelter Repair Scorecard (55 columns), and years of unsheltered street-count history.

But there is NO service layer to expose — applying for shelter is in-person and reporting a person on the street for outreach is a NYC311 'Homeless Person Assistance' call; neither has a machine-readable contract, and DHS does not even own the 311 channel that stands in for one.
People are published only in aggregate (shelter census and street counts); DHS never exposes an individual record to Open Data.

Net-new write surface: request street outreach (OutreachRequest) — reporting a person experiencing unsheltered homelessness so an outreach team responds, with no API today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 23 DHS datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open DHS API — observational/reference data only.
'Homeless Person Assistance' street-outreach reports and shelter lookups — where the DHS transaction actually happens. Owned by 311, not DHS; no documented DHS API.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM, livesite-version: 22. No content API.

fruit.name: Daily Shelter Census
opendata_match: Daily Report Of Single Adult And Family Intake sci4-yqgk
Directory Of Homeless Population By Year 5t4n-d72c
fruit.notes: The flagship DHS asset — the daily count of people in the NYC shelter system by client population (single adults, adult families, families with children). DHS's single most-viewed dataset.

fruit.name: Homeless Drop-In Centers
opendata_match: Directory Of Homeless Drop-In Centers bmxf-3rd4 (13c)
fruit.notes: 24/7 sites for people experiencing street homelessness — address, comments, full NYC geography spine and lat/long.

fruit.name: Shelter Facilities & Repair Scorecard
opendata_match: Buildings by Borough and Community District 3qem-6v3v
Individual Census by Borough, Community District, and Facility Type veav-vj3r
Associated Address by Borough and Community District ur7y-ziyb
fruit.notes: Shelter buildings by DHS Building ID — landlord, capacity, facility-type census, and open/new/closed housing-maintenance violations by priority and inspecting agency (HPD, DOB, FDNY, DOHMH).

fruit.name: DHS Contacts & Intake Centers
fruit.notes: DHS offices, intake centers, and public contact points — including where New Yorkers apply for shelter (PATH family intake, adult intake). Phone, address, geography spine.

fruit.name: Unsheltered Street Counts
opendata_match: Unsheltered Street Homeless To General Population Ratio 2012 483x-fy9e (13c)
LL19 Quarterly Unsheltered Street Homeless Individuals 7tu6-bcih
fruit.notes: Aggregate counts of unsheltered/street-homeless individuals and their ratio to the general population — AGGREGATE ONLY. No individual person is ever published. The read-side twin of the net-new OutreachRequest write surface.

fruit.name: Apply for shelter
fruit.notes: Applying for shelter is in-person only (PATH for families with children; adult-intake centers). No API. An alternative net-new write surface (ShelterIntakeRequest).

fruit.name: Report a person for outreach
fruit.notes: The single most time-sensitive DHS interaction — report a person sleeping on the street so a mobile outreach team responds, and track it. No API and no Open Data twin; today only a NYC311 'Homeless Person Assistance' call (911 for emergencies). The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.
